# split_h_2p_demo -DROTATE — Audit Protocol audit-2 (remediation verification)

**Increment audited:** `claude/persp-c-horiz-review-ntqlca` @ `58d7343` (split_h_2p_demo -DROTATE). **Remediation/hardening commit under review:** `2b6066d` (HEAD). **Audit-1 report:** `asm_repo_staging/docs/audit/split_h_2p_demo-rotate-audit-1.md` (CLEAN, 3 info findings).
**Role:** independent AUDIT-2 — I did not build the increment, did not write audit-1, did not write the hardening. All evidence below regenerated by me from a fresh materialization (`dryrun_split.sh /tmp/kit_audit2r` at 2b6066d); mutant assembled in a scratch copy (`/tmp/kit_audit2r_mutant`), never in the repo. `/home/user/SuperForge` untouched throughout (`git status` clean, HEAD = 2b6066d at close).

## VERDICT: REMEDIATION VERIFIED

The commit is exactly the audit-1 report plus the two claimed hardenings (3 files, +119/-0, nothing else). The binding assertion's addresses and formula are correct against the ASM and the link map, its discrimination was demonstrated positively (the exact mix-up audit-1 hypothesized fails the hardened test at step 1) **and** differentially (the pre-hardening test passes that same mutant — the new assertion alone carries the load). The compile-time guard fires; all six shipped variants still build. 18/18 + four gates clean from fresh materialization; the hardened cadence test passed 3 independent runs (no flake from the added per-step reads). Cleanroom spot-check of report + commit text: clean.

## Per-finding disposition

| # | Audit-1 finding (info) | Claimed remediation | Audit-2 disposition |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Cadence/render tests don't pin pointer↔heading *binding*; both-bands-from-H1 mix-up would pass | Cadence gate asserts `ptr == $8000 + h·448` per band (band 1 ← H1, band 2 ← H2) at every step | **VERIFIED — correct and discriminating.** Addresses cross-checked: `IDX_AB=$C000`, `IDX_CD=$C010` (index-table `[cnt,ptr][cnt,ptr][0]` → ptr words at +1/+4, so $C001/$C011/$C004/$C014), `H1=$C06A`, `H2=$C06C` — all match `main.asm:117-128`; store targets match (`main.asm` band 1 → +1 from H1, band 2 → +4 from H2). Blob loword: BANK2/BANK3 segments load at ROM2/ROM3 = `$028000`/`$038000` (`lorom_stream.cfg:30-31`, segment decls `main.asm:728-731`) → `.loword = $8000` for both, additionally confirmed at runtime by 72 passing per-step formula reads. Max offset 63·448+$8000 = $EE40, no 16-bit carry; `& 0xFFFF` harmless. Mutation proof below. |
| 2 | ROTATE/RETARGET exclusion comment- and script-enforced only | Compile-time `.error` when both defined | **VERIFIED.** `main.asm:81-85` `.ifdef ROTATE`/`.ifdef RETARGET` → `.error`. Hand build with `-DROTATE=1 -DRETARGET=1` fails at line 83 with the user error, exit 1. All six shipped variants (`_freeze`, `_sameorigin`, `_retarget`, `_latch`, `_rotate`, `_rotfreeze`) still build (64 KB ×4, 512 KB ×2). |
| 3 | `lorom_stream.cfg` banner comment describes only the streaming rail's BANK2/BANK3 use | Deferred to next touch (file untouched by increment) | **DEFERRAL ACCEPTED.** Confirmed the cfg is untouched by both 58d7343 and 2b6066d, banner still says "flat tilemap — STREAMS in" (cfg lines 10-11). Info-severity comment nit in an unedited shared file; deferring is proportionate and the rationale is accurate. |

## Evidence log (all regenerated by me at 2b6066d)

Full diff read: report file is new; main.asm hunk is exactly the 5-line guard + blank; test hunk is exactly the 4-comment-line + 2-read + 4-assert binding block inside `test_rot_cadence_true_60fps_in_situ`'s step loop (new locals `h1`/`h2` do not shadow the pre-existing `h1_0`/`h2_0`/`h` used by the surrounding asserts). Nothing else. 2b6066d sits directly on 58d7343.

**2. Fresh materialization, suite, gates, flake check** (`/tmp/kit_audit2r`):

```
$ bash asm_repo_staging/tools/dryrun_split.sh /tmp/kit_audit2r
scrub_split: OK — 71 substitutions across 18 files; comment lineage guard clean
$ make split_h_2p_demo && bash templates/split_h_2p_demo/build_split_h_2p_variants.sh
built build/split_h_2p_demo.sfc (cfg=lorom_64k.cfg)
built ..._freeze/_sameorigin/_retarget/_latch (65536 B each)
built ..._rotate.sfc (ROTATE=1) / _rotfreeze.sfc (ROTATE=1 FREEZE=1)  [524288 B each]
$ python -m pytest tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py tests/test_gen_pose_tables.py -q
18 passed in 20.93s
$ bash tools/cleanroom_check.sh ; make width-check ; make zp-check ; make provenance-check
cleanroom: clean · width-check: clean (189 files) · zp_lint: 0 finding(s) across 230 file(s)
provenance: clean — 126 blob(s) accounted for (92 generated, 6 third-party, 9 attested, 19 artifact)
$ pytest tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py::test_rot_cadence_true_60fps_in_situ -q   (extra run 1)
1 passed in 1.65s
$ pytest tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py::test_rot_cadence_true_60fps_in_situ -q   (extra run 2)
1 passed in 1.65s
```

3 total passes of the hardened cadence test (suite + 2 solo) — the added 6 reads/step introduce no flake (reads occur while frame-stepped, no timing exposure).

**3. Guard check** (exact variant-script invocation, forbidden combo):

```
$ ca65 --cpu 65816 -I infrastructure/rom_template -I lib/macros -I engine \
    -I templates/split_h_2p_demo/assets -DROTATE=1 -DRETARGET=1 \
    templates/split_h_2p_demo/main.asm -o <scratch>/guard_test.o
templates/split_h_2p_demo/main.asm(83): Error: User error: ROTATE and RETARGET both own band 2's pose pointers — do not combine
EXIT=1
```

**4. Mix-up mutation — discrimination demonstrated** (scratch copy `/tmp/kit_audit2r_mutant`; mutation = `main.asm:543` band-2 pointer-recompute load `H2` → `H1`, i.e. both bands' pointers driven from H1 at H1's rate/sense, exactly audit-1's hypothesized bug; headings/movement untouched):

```
$ sed -i '543s/H2/H1/' templates/split_h_2p_demo/main.asm   # lda f:$7E0000 + H1
$ python -m pytest tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py::test_rot_cadence_true_60fps_in_situ -q
E   AssertionError: band-2 AB pointer not bound to H2 at step 1
E   assert 39040 == ((32768 + (23 * 448)) & 65535)
1 failed in 1.45s
```

Decode: actual pointer $9880 = $8000 + **14**·448 = f(H1=14); expected $A840 = f(H2=23) — the mutant's band-2 pointer demonstrably tracks H1 and the binding assertion catches it on the first step. Differential control — the **pre-hardening** test (extracted verbatim from `58d7343`) run against the *same mutant ROM*:

```
$ git show 58d7343:asm_repo_staging/tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py > tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py
$ python -m pytest tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py::test_rot_cadence_true_60fps_in_situ -q
1 passed in 1.72s
```

The old change-only guards pass the mix-up (confirming audit-1's gap was real); the new binding assertion is the discriminating addition.

**5. Cleanroom spot-check** (audit-1 report + 2b6066d commit message): the report is materialized into the kit (`/tmp/kit_audit2r/docs/audit/split_h_2p_demo-rotate-audit-1.md`), so the passing cleanroom gate already scanned it; additionally I ran the gate's own lineage regex, its full commercial/company denylist, and an extended vendor-chip trade-name list over the concatenated report + commit text: **zero hits** on all three scans.

**Files referenced:** `asm_repo_staging/templates/split_h_2p_demo/main.asm` (guard :81-85; WRAM layout :117-130; pointer recompute :521-563), `asm_repo_staging/tests/test_split_h_2p_demo.py` (binding block :321-335), `asm_repo_staging/infrastructure/rom_template/lorom_stream.cfg` (ROM2/ROM3 :30-31), `asm_repo_staging/docs/audit/split_h_2p_demo-rotate-audit-1.md`.
